read_plan.preview: This hub indexes named characters across [[Solumora]] — the working population who make the world's systems function, and the specific individuals whose lives intersect with the world's larger tensions.
The people at the center of the world's main threads.
**[[Selvane]]** — In Solhaven. Infrastructure consultant, Crestward-adjacent. Does not know Mave is also in the city.
**[[Ren Valdh]]** (T5) — Council administrative officer. Knows more than council members would prefer.

The numeric tier is the spell's **Flux handling requirement**. Each tier is roughly an order-of-magnitude jump in difficulty, capacity, stability, and precision. Every few tiers feels like a categorically different class
_Barely any Flux. The threshold most people never bother to formally test._
_A small, usable trickle. The first tier where deliberate spellwork is practical._
_The world average. The first tier where magic becomes genuinely useful in daily life._

Auralis society leans toward a soft caste structure, where social standing is loosely tied to magical ability. This is not a rigid hierarchy of law — high-tier casters do not hold formal political office by virtue of the

Auralis embraces the full spectrum of [[Discipline|Disciplines]], including those that make other cultures uncomfortable. Experimentation with Mind and Soul disciplines is not openly banned and in certain scholarly or mi

The principal settlements of Auralis are [[Solhaven]] (the capital, built up coastal terraces on the western shore) and [[Emberfall]] (the interior research and agricultural center). Auralis has several additional port c
Solhaven approaches a million residents and is the most vertically stratified city in Solumora. Its terrace zones function as distinct social worlds stacked on each other.

Terravelle society values stability and efficiency above all. Where other cultures may chase magical prestige, Terravelle embraces the philosophy that the best spell is the one that accomplishes the task with the least e

Low to mid-tier casting is common and unremarkable. [[Grimoires]] here tend to favor Triangle and Square sigil shapes — fast, safe, reproducible, and affordable in [[Flux]]. [[Discipline|Disciplines]] with predictable fa

[[Terravelle]] and [[Auralis]] share a complicated relationship shaped by fundamentally opposed philosophies. Where Auralis pursues magical innovation and supremacy, Terravelle prizes restraint and function. The [[Equato

The principal settlements of Terravelle are [[Valdenmoor]] (the capital, in the central river valley), [[Ashford]] (the primary eastern port), and [[Greyveil]] (the northern waypoint at the edge of the settled belt). Doz

Solhaven is organized by elevation in ways that are officially geographic and practically social. The harbor district at the base is commercial — trade, warehousing, transport, the practical work of a port city. As you m

[[Cassia]]'s palace occupies the Crestward's eastern face, designed to catch morning light and be visible from the harbor. It is large, ornate, and functions as both a residence and a performance space — the formal halls

Solhaven's population approaches a million. It is the largest city in Solumora and the most [[Flux]]-saturated — the density of active [[Sigils]] in the upper terraces is high enough that visitors with [[Control Tier]] s

[[Religion and Belief|The Ascendant Path]] runs deeper in Solhaven than anywhere else in [[Auralis]]. The Luminants — the Path's formal priesthood — operate out of three major temples in the city, the largest of which si

A transaction between two registered individuals requires the consent of both parties — one initiates, one confirms, and the ledger writes. The process takes moments for anyone with basic Flux sensitivity and slightly lo

The Watts ledger predates both kingdoms. The earliest records of birth rune transactions in either kingdom's archives trace back further than coherent written history — the system was already running when the first settl

Some people carry physical tokens stamped with their birth rune mark as a convenience — not because the token holds value, but because presenting it speeds up the consent verification process in busy commercial environme

Watts are fiat currency in the strict sense — they are backed by nothing except the network's integrity and the collective agreement to treat them as valuable. There is no gold reserve, no Flux commodity, no state guaran

Valdenmoor follows the Terravellian architectural instinct without compromise — the city spreads outward rather than upward, a sprawl of single and double-story buildings connected by wide roads designed to move goods ef

Valdenmoor is the largest city in [[Terravelle]] and one of the largest in [[Solumora]], with a population somewhere above half a million. It draws from every region of the kingdom — merchants, [[Flux Users|Scribes]], cr

The Maren River runs through the center of the city and has been the reason for Valdenmoor's location since the original settlement. River trade along the Maren connects the capital to the eastern and western coasts, and

[[Cael]] grew up in Valdenmoor and spends most of his time here. The parties, the sympathetic [[Flux Users|Formulists]] he questions about channeling cost, the social world he moves through — all of it is Valdenmoor. Edd

Ashford is rougher than [[Valdenmoor]] in the way that port cities tend to be — more transient, more tolerant of the irregular, more oriented toward the transaction than the institution. [[Flux]] use here leans heavily t

The coastal territory north of Ashford — absorbed into [[Terravelle]] during the campaign in which Eddan's elder son died — has never fully settled into the kingdom's administration. The independent coastal communities t

Eddan considers Ashford one of the three non-negotiables of Terravellian security. Losing control of the eastern coast would cut off trade revenue that funds a significant portion of the kingdom's military and administra

Ashford's trade volume depends on three movement systems that overlap without merging cleanly. Bulk cargo arrives through the [[Maren Freight Chain]] and is redistributed through dock districts that can absorb schedule s

- Magic fundamentals: [[Flux]], [[Control Tier]], [[Sigils]], [[Hypertext]], [[Hand Signs]]

Halveth's permanent population is small — a core of perhaps sixty to eighty people who live there year-round. Guides, suppliers, innkeepers, a small number of families who have been in the area long enough that leaving s

Halveth's economy is almost entirely built on outfitting desert crossings. Thermal [[Sigils|sigil]] sets, preserved food rated for extreme temperature variance, navigation equipment, replacement grimoire covers treated a
[[Wren]] has been a fixture of Halveth for thirty years. She does not hold any formal title. She does not need one.

Halveth's proximity to the desert means it sits closer to active [[Rift Incursions|rift zones]] than any other permanent settlement in [[Solumora]]. Lower-order [[Flux Demons]] reach the village's outskirts with some reg

Wolfpoint was founded by the Voss family — a lineage of soldier scholars who had spent careers inscribing [[Sigils]] onto armour, blades, and field equipment, and grown deeply frustrated with what the sigil system could

Wolfpoint retains its military heritage in everything. The settlement is organized around discipline and function — buildings are low and wind-resistant, layouts are practical, nothing is built for display. The Voss line
Wolfpoint knows [[Terravelle]] and [[Auralis]] exist. It trades with both, maintains occasional correspondence, and has no interest in being absorbed by either.

What Wolfpoint has built over seven generations is not a finished system. [[Hypertext]] is still being developed — new symbol sequences are being tested, failure modes are being catalogued, and the limits of what a linea

Emberfall's research identity depends on field systems that are less visible than laboratories but equally decisive. Council-backed desert work now runs through a practical chain: permitting and compliance at [[Emberfall

A scholar at [[Emberfall]] commits themselves to mastery of one thing. Some choose a specific [[Sigils|sigil]] — they will spend years refining it, expanding its applications, understanding its boundaries in ways that in

Emberfall scholars do not conduct pure research in the way theoretical mages might. Their work is immediately applied. A scholar working on a combat sigil conducts experiments in controlled environments, documents result

[[Emberfall]] is led by a director appointed (in practice) by whoever on the [[The Council of Auralis|Council]] has strongest interest in [[Flux]] advancement — currently [[Drest]] and the Culmination Faction. The direct
